content.problemStatement:
There are NNN different colours of balloons, numbered 111 to NNN. The iii-th colour balloon costs exactly iii rupees. That is, a balloon of colour 111 costs 111 rupee, balloon of colour 222 costs 222 rupees and so on.

You bought AiA_iAi​ balloons of the iii-th colour, what is the total cost of all the balloons you bought?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN - the number of colours.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the total cost of the balloons.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : You have 555 balloons of colour 111, so a total cost of 5⋅15 \cdot 15⋅1.
Test Case 2 : 111 balloon each of colours 111, 222, and 333, thus a cost of 1+2+3=61 + 2 + 3 = 61+2+3=6.
